reasoning: The Lean signature quantifies universally over a topological space X and a closed subset A, assumes X is normal via Mathlib's `NormalSpace` class (which asserts disjoint closed sets have disjoint open neighborhoods — the standard definition), and concludes that the subtype `{x // x ∈ A}` (which inherits the subspace topology via the canonical instance) is a normal space. This exactly matches "a closed subspace of a normal space is normal." The encoding of "closed subspace" as a subtype of a closed set is standard in Mathlib, and the hypothesis/conclusion symmetry preserves the meaning of the claim. No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what the informal statement requires.
